Digital Service Standard

The Digital Service Standard defines 10 criteria that government digital services must meet. This page tracks how AGDS-vue aligns with each criterion, and maps them to automated assertions in our Vitest + axe-core test suite.

16 / 35 checklist items done
1

Understand what the problem is

In progress

Component APIs are deliberate, minimal, and derived from real government service patterns.

  • ✓Every prop has a clearly stated purpose in TSDoc
  • ✓Prop names are consistent across the library (variant, size, disabled, loading)
  • ○All exported types documented with use cases
  • ○Breaking changes require migration guide
2

Understand what users need

Not started

Props, slots, and events reflect real-world patterns found in Australian government service forms.

  • ○All form components support v-model binding
  • ○Label, hint, and error message are first-class props on every form component
  • ○Components support server-side validation error display
3

Leave no one behind

In progress

Every component meets WCAG 2.2 AA. Reka UI handles keyboard navigation and ARIA.

  • ○axe-core passes on all components in all states
  • ○WCAG 2.2 AA contrast ratios met (4.5:1 text, 3:1 large/UI)
  • ✓Focus ring: 3px solid #9263de on all interactive elements
  • ✓Reka UI primitives provide keyboard nav for all interactive components
  • ✓Button: aria-busy on loading, aria-disabled on disabled
4

Use agile and user-centred methods

In progress

Components compose cleanly — slots over props, small focused components over monoliths.

  • ✓Button exposes default slot for content
  • ○Layout components expose named slots for structural regions
  • ○No component reaches into its parent or uses $parent
5

Understand what can be reused

Complete

All style values reference --agds-* CSS custom properties. No hardcoded colours or spacing.

  • ✓Every colour value references a --agds-* CSS custom property
  • ✓Every spacing value references a --agds-space-* token
  • ✓Every font value references --agds-font-* tokens
  • ✓No component uses !important
  • ✓No component uses Tailwind or any external CSS class framework
6

Describe, not prescribe

In progress

Reka UI primitives used for all interaction patterns — no custom focus traps or ARIA management.

  • ✓All interactive component primitives documented in COMPONENTS.md
  • ○No component reimplements focus trapping or keyboard routing that Reka provides
  • ✓reka-ui pinned as a peer dependency
7

Ensure the service is simple to use

Complete

Zero runtime dependencies beyond vue and reka-ui. No analytics, no CDN fonts.

  • ✓Zero runtime dependencies beyond vue and reka-ui
  • ✓No analytics, telemetry, or external network calls
  • ✓No CDN font or icon loading — system font stack, inline SVG slots
8

Make the service accountable

Not started

Reka UI keeps primitives modern and maintained. Changelog maintained per Conventional Commits.

  • ○Reka UI peer dependency kept up to date
  • ○CHANGELOG.md maintained per Conventional Commits
  • ○Each component's Reka primitive documented with reason it was chosen
9

Be open

Not started

Minimum 80% statement coverage. Every component has render, slot, props, events, and a11y tests.

  • ○Minimum 80% statement coverage
  • ○Every component: render, slot, props, events, and axe-core tests
  • ○Coverage report on every PR
10

Keep improving

Not started

Semantic Versioning, deprecation warnings, and migration guides for every breaking change.

  • ○Semantic Versioning strictly followed
  • ○Deprecated props annotated with @deprecated and console warning in dev
  • ○Migration guides for every breaking change
